what_it_does: CSS that frames the board-view overlay modal: a fixed-position scrim, a centered flex panel, header, scrollable body, message/error/quiet states, a retry pill, and overrides that force DNA's grouped board content to render full-width in a single column.
observations: Uses CSS custom-property tokens for spacing, color, typography, radius, elevation, and safe areas.
Modal is toggled via .board-overlay--open using opacity, visibility, and pointer-events transitions.
Panel is capped at 760px max-width and full viewport max-height, with a flex column layout.
Header contains a title, subtitle, and a close button with a 44px touch target.
Body scrolls and adds bottom safe-area padding; the groups container is full width and responsive SVGs scale.
Contains a long rationale comment explaining why it overrides DNA's .screen and .ar-responsive classes.
Overrides reset a fixed 390px phone-frame width and a desktop two-column grid to a single-column flex layout.
standouts: The override block explicitly neutralizes DNA's fixed 390px phone-frame and .ar-responsive desktop 2-col grid so the board list fills the panel.
Comments read like a forensic design log, citing a specific issue found at 1440px.
Despite the 'tokens only — no bespoke values' claim, the close button uses hardcoded 15px font-size and 44px min dimensions.
conventions: Follows a token-driven design system via CSS custom properties.
Follows the common overlay pattern: fixed inset, scrim, centered panel, open state class.
Breaks its own token-only convention with a few magic numbers for the close button and border widths.
concerns: Hardcoded 15px and 44px on .board-close conflict with the stated 'tokens only' rule.
Comments reference external files and upstream behavior that could drift.
.board-groups svg rule may affect all SVGs inside the board regardless of intended sizing.
.board-overlay justify-content is not 'safe', though align-items uses safe center.
notes: This file is the framing shell for a scrollable, full-width board overlay and includes defensive overrides to keep DNA's grouped board from collapsing into a phone-frame or two-column grid.
questions: Where are DNA's boardGroups styles defined and what classes do they expose?
What do .ar, .ar-responsive, and .ar-zone-* normally do outside this file?
Are the hardcoded close-button values intentional exceptions or oversights?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to frame a full-width, scrollable board overlay and override conflicting responsive layouts; the CSS does exactly that.
intention_signals: Present a grouped board as a long, full-width single-column list inside a centered modal, while neutralizing the underlying design system's fixed phone-frame and two-column responsive grid.
novelty: The extensive rationale comments act as a design decision log, including a specific bug discovery at 1440px, and the file makes an explicit 'tokens only' claim while still using some magic numbers.
contribution: Provides the modal shell and layout overrides that allow the board view to be a scrollable, full-width, single-column list rather than a fixed-width phone frame or two-column grid.
summary_for_embedding: CSS for a centered, scrollable board overlay modal with scrim, header, body, message states, and overrides that force DNA's grouped board content to render full-width in a single column instead of a fixed phone frame or two-column responsive grid.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to frame the board view as a long, full-width grouped list inside a modal while neutralizing conflicting responsive layouts from the underlying design system.
